title: श्री विष्णु आरती
titleEn: Vishnu Aarti - Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु आरती ॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा, स्वामी जय लक्ष्मी रमणा।
श्रीविष्णु नारायण, पतितपावन हरि नारायणा॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

चरण कमल में चारों वेद, ब्रह्मा जी ध्यावे।
कपिल, सनक, सनन्दन, शेषनाग गुण गावे॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

शंख चक्र गदा पद्मधारी, गरुड़ पर सवारी।
पीताम्बर शोभित, वनमाला बनवारी॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

क्षीरसिन्धु में विराजत, शेषशैय्या पर सोये।
लक्ष्मी चरण दबावे, ब्रह्मा ध्यान जो होये॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

नरसिंह रूप धर कर, हिरण्यकश्यपु मारा।
प्रहलाद बचाया, जगत में यश तुम्हारा॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

दशरथ सुत श्रीरामचन्द्र, लंका दल मारा।
सीता सहित राजतिलक, आनन्द भयो अपारा॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

श्रीकृष्ण रूप धर कर, कंस को मारा।
द्रौपदी चीर बढ़ाया, जग में यश तुम्हारा॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

श्रीविष्णु जी की आरती, जो कोई नर गावे।
कहत शिवानन्द स्वामी, सुख सम्पत्ति पावे॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Aarti ||

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana, Swami Jai Lakshmi Ramana.
Shri Vishnu Narayan, Patitpavan Hari Narayana.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Charan Kamal Mein Charon Ved, Brahma Ji Dhyave.
Kapil, Sanak, Sanandan, Sheshnag Gun Gaave.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Shankh Chakra Gada Padmadhari, Garud Par Sawari.
Peetambar Shobhit, Vanmala Banwari.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Kshirsindhu Mein Virajat, Sheshshaiyya Par Soye.
Lakshmi Charan Dabave, Brahma Dhyan Jo Hoye.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Narsingh Roop Dhar Kar, Hiranyakashyapu Mara.
Prahlad Bachaya, Jagat Mein Yash Tumhara.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Dashrath Sut Shri Ramchandra, Lanka Dal Mara.
Sita Sahit Rajtilak, Anand Bhayo Apara.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Shri Krishna Roop Dhar Kar, Kans Ko Mara.
Draupadi Cheer Badhaya, Jag Mein Yash Tumhara.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Shri Vishnu Ji Ki Aarti, Jo Koi Nar Gaave.
Kahat Shivanand Swami, Sukh Sampatti Paave.

Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी के रमण! हे स्वामी! श्रीविष्णु नारायण, पतितपावन हरि नारायण!

चरणकमलों में चारों वेद हैं, ब्रह्माजी ध्यान करते हैं। कपिल, सनक, सनन्दन और शेषनाग गुण गाते हैं।

शंख, चक्र, गदा और पद्म धारण करने वाले, गरुड़ पर सवारी करने वाले। पीताम्बर शोभित है, वनमाला पहने बनवारी।

क्षीरसागर में विराजमान हैं, शेषशैय्या पर शयन करते हैं। लक्ष्मी चरण दबाती हैं, ब्रह्मा ध्यान करते हैं।

नरसिंह रूप धारण करके हिरण्यकश्यपु को मारा। प्रह्लाद को बचाया, जगत में तुम्हारा यश है।

दशरथ पुत्र श्रीरामचन्द्र ने लंका के दल को मारा। सीता सहित राजतिलक हुआ, अपार आनन्द हुआ।

श्रीकृष्ण रूप धारण करके कंस को मारा। द्रौपदी का चीर बढ़ाया, जग में तुम्हारा यश है।

श्रीविष्णु जी की यह आरती जो कोई मनुष्य गाता है, शिवानन्द स्वामी कहते हैं — वह सुख-सम्पत्ति पाता है।
benefits: Traditional aarti of Lord Vishnu describing His major avatars
Invokes the blessings of all Vishnu avatars - Narasimha, Rama, Krishna
Traditionally associated with protection, prosperity, and grace of lakshmi
Ideal for Thursday worship and all Vishnu puja occasions
Invokes success and removes all obstacles from life
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Vishnu Aarti - Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana (श्री विष्णु आरती) is a traditional worship song (aarti) revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Traditional Aarti Sangrah, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to bring protection, prosperity, and grace of Lakshmi, invoke the blessings of all Vishnu avatars - Narasimha, Rama, Krishna, and grant success and remove all obstacles from life. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Devuthani Ekadashi and Anant Chaturdashi. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Aarti - Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Vishnu Aarti in Hindi with meaning - Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana. Traditional aarti of Lord Vishnu describing His avatars for daily worship.
relatedSearches: Om Jai Jagdish Hare lyrics in Hindi

seoFaqs.question: Who composed 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare'?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare' was composed by Pandit Shraddha Ram Phillauri (1837–1881), a Sanskrit scholar, kathavachak, social reformer, and writer from Phillaur in Punjab. He is credited with composing this aarti as well as the parallel 'Om Jai Lakshmi Mata' so that Sanatana Dharma's core teachings could be sung in simple Hindustani by everyday worshippers. The aarti became the most widely sung Hindu devotional song of the modern era and is heard in Krishna and Vishnu temples across the world. (Source: Sutharsamaj; Hindupedia; Idobhakti.)

seoFaqs.question: When is this Vishnu Aarti sung?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare' is sung daily during morning and evening puja in countless Hindu households and temples. It is sung at the close of Satyanarayan Vrat Katha, after Vishnu Sahasranama recitation, on every Ekadashi, on Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha or Pausha), Devshayani and Devotthana Ekadashi, Akshaya Tritiya, Janmashtami, Ram Navami, Govardhan Puja, and on Thursdays (Brihaspativar), the day traditionally associated with Vishnu in the planetary scheme. It also concludes most Vaishnava bhajan-mandalis. (Source: Hindupedia; Drik Panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Jagdish Hare' mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Jagdish is a compound of jagat (world) and isha (lord), meaning 'Lord of the World,' an epithet of Vishnu as the supreme sustainer of the universe. Hari is another name of Vishnu meaning 'one who removes (sins, sorrows, illusions).' The opening invocation 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare' therefore translates as 'Victory to the Lord of the World, Hari, the remover of sorrows.' The aarti goes on to enumerate Vishnu's qualities as protector of devotees, granter of boons, and remover of distress. (Source: Stotra Rathna translations; Vedkosh.)

seoFaqs.question: How is this aarti related to other Phillauri aartis?
seoFaqs.answer: Pandit Shraddha Ram Phillauri composed both 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare' (Vishnu) and 'Om Jai Lakshmi Mata' (Lakshmi) using the same metrical structure, the same congregational refrain pattern, and the same accessible Hindustani vocabulary. The two aartis are commonly sung in sequence during Lakshmi Puja on Diwali and at Satyanarayan Katha. The model has been so influential that countless other deities (Durga, Saraswati, Kali, Shiva, Hanuman) now have aartis composed in the same 'Om Jai...' format inspired by Phillauri's template. (Source: Hindupedia; Vedic Stories.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees attribute to this aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The verses themselves promise that the troubles of devotees and the sufferings of servants are removed in a moment by Lord Vishnu, that the mind ceases to wander when fixed on him, that all virtues are attained through his grace, and that material and spiritual fulfilment flow to those who sing his praise. Devotees recite it as a daily expression of sharanagati (surrender) and as a closing prayer that gathers all the merit of preceding worship and offers it to Vishnu. (Source: Idobhakti; My Jyotish.)
